title: Create a change log download task
content:
Use this endpoint to initiate a change log download task for an ad account based on the parameters that you specify. ## Comparing v1.2 and v1.3 The following table outlines the differences between v1.2 and v1.3 endpoints. ```xtable |Changes{30%}|v1.2{35%}|v1.3{35%}| |---|---|---| |Endpoint path|/v1.2/activities/task/create/|/v1.3/changelog/task/create/| |Request parameter name|`opt_types`
`order_field`|`operation_types`
`order_fields`| |Request parameter data type |`advertiser_id`:number
`order_fields`:string|`advertiser_id`: string
`order_fields `:string[]| |Request parameters deprecated in v1.3|/|`page`
`page_size`| ``` ## Request **Endpoint** https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/changelog/task/create/ **Method** POST **Header** ``` xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |Access-Token {Required}|string|Authorized access token. For details, see [Authentication](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1738373164380162).| |Content-Type {Required}|string|Request message type.
Allowed values: `"application/json"`. | ``` **Parameters** ``` xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |advertiser_id {Required}|string| Advertiser ID | |start_time |string| Query start time, in the format of `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`.
Example: `2025-09-01 00:00:00`. | |end_time |string| Query end time, in the format of `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`
Example: `2025-09-01 00:00:00`.

Note: The largest time gap allowed is 30 days, so please specify a date within 30 days after the start_time as end_time. | |timezone |string| Timezone of returned value, refer to [Appendix-Time Zone](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737586324313089) for optional values. | |module |string| Module to be queried, optional values:

  • `BIDDING_AND_OPTIMIZATION`
  • `BUDGET`
  • `STATUS`
  • `TARGETING`
| |object_ids |string[]| Query object ID | |object_type |string| Query object type, optional values: `AD`,`ADGROUP`,`ADVERTISER`,`CAMPAIGN`,`INSTANT_FORM` | |operation_types |string[]| Operation type, optional values: `CREATE`, `AUDIT`, `STATUS`, `UPDATE`, `DELETE`, `DOWNLOAD_LEADS`, `SUBSCRIBE_FORM`, `UNSUBSCRIBE_FORM` | |order_fields |string[]| Order by fields | ``` ### Example ``` xcodeblock (code curl http) curl -H "Access-Token:xxx" -H "Content-Type:application/json" -X POST \ -d '{ "order_type": "ORDER_TYPE", "order_fields": "ORDER_FIELDS", "operation_types": [ "OPT_TYPES" ], "start_time": "START_TIME", "object_type": "OBJECT_TYPE", "object_ids": [ "OBJECT_IDS" ], "module": "MODULE", "advertiser_id": "ADVERTISER_ID", "end_time": "END_TIME", "timezone": "TIMEZONE", }' \ https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/changelog/task/create/ (/code) ``` ## Response ``` xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |code |number|Return code, see [Appendix-Return code](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097) | |message |string|Return messages, see [Appendix-Return information](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097)| |data |object|Return data| #|task_id |string| Task ID | |request_id |string|The log id of a request, which uniquely identifies the request.| ``` ### Example ``` xcodeblock (code Success-Response http) HTTPS/1.1 200 OK { "code": 0, "data": { "task_id": "6917870916856709125" }, "message": "OK" } (/code); ```
